prerequisites: A Recurly account with access to a Recurly site (the merchant account whose subscription and billing data you want to read).
A user role that includes the Integration permission, which is required to view and create API credentials under Integrations > API Credentials.
A private API key generated for the Recurly site (the api_key value), since every Recurly REST API request is authenticated with this key.
If reading from a Recurly sandbox site rather than production, note that the connector exposes an is_sandbox config field to indicate the environment.

authMethods.name: Private API key (HTTP Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the single required secret field api_key, your Recurly private API key. Recurly authenticates every REST API request using this private key over HTTP Basic Authentication, where the key is supplied as the Basic auth credential. Generate the key in the Recurly Admin Dashboard under Integrations > API Credentials by choosing Add Private API Key.

setupSteps.title: Generate a private API key in Recurly
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Recurly Admin Dashboard with a user whose role includes the Integration permission. Go to Integrations > API Credentials and choose Add Private API Key. Assign the key a name and note its purpose, then save. Copy the generated private key; this is the value for the connector's api_key field.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the required api_key config field to the private API key you generated. Recurly sends this key as an HTTP Basic Authentication credential on every API request, so the connector uses it to read your account, subscription, and billing data. Keep the key secret: never commit it to version control or embed it in client-side code.

setupSteps.title: Select the site environment
setupSteps.body: If you are connecting to a Recurly sandbox site for testing rather than your live production site, set the optional is_sandbox config field accordingly. Make sure the api_key you provide belongs to the same site you are targeting.

setupSteps.title: Configure the extraction window and tuning options (optional)
setupSteps.body: Use the optional begin_time and end_time fields to bound the time range of records the connector reads. Set accounts_step_days to control the day-size of the windows used when paging through accounts, and num_workers to control how many parallel workers the connector uses during extraction.
